friction_items.description: 222 unique MCP tool names. A naive AI on turn 1 cannot meaningfully triage this without sys_active_tools, but sys_active_tools requires already knowing the protocol. tool_audit_* alone has >20 variants (tool_audit_assumptions, tool_audit_citations, tool_audit_claims, tool_audit_cliches, tool_audit_code_quality, tool_audit_coherence, tool_audit_cross_deliverable_consistency, tool_audit_dashboard_content, tool_audit_evalue, tool_audit_figure, tool_audit_figure_coverage, tool_audit_figure_full, tool_audit_figure_interactivity, tool_audit_figure_quality, tool_audit_findings_diff, tool_audit_findings_query, tool_audit_power, tool_audit_prose, tool_audit_quality_full, tool_audit_reproducibility, tool_audit_reviewer_responses, tool_audit_statistical_power, tool_audit_step_completeness, tool_audit_step_literature, tool_audit_synthesis, tool_audit_version_coherence). A naive AI cannot distinguish tool_audit_claims from tool_audit_citations from tool_audit_evalue without reading each full description.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Phase 9 consolidation: collapse the audit family into tool_audit_run(dimension='claims|prose|figures|...') with one dispatcher; same for the dashboard_* and synthesis_* clusters. Surface a 'starter shortlist' of ~20 core tools by default; gate the long tail behind explicit category requests.

friction_items.description: The engineering pack (FMEA, FTA, requirements traceability, build_test_fix_loop, test_failure_causation, design_iteration, engineering_report_structure) is overwhelmingly safety-engineering / V&V-engineering / industrial-controls flavored. The detector keys on CAD files, .plc, .scada, modbus. For an algorithmic/runtime benchmark of 3 algorithms x 5 sizes x 10 runs, NONE of the pack's protocols or tools (fmea_render, fault_tree_render, requirements_matrix) are useful. There is no engineering/benchmark/runtime_benchmark.yaml or tool_runtime_measure. A naive AI looking at the pack will conclude 'wrong domain' and fall back to methodology/method_comparison.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Either (a) add engineering/test/runtime_benchmark.yaml + tool_runtime_aggregate (warm-up handling, paired wilcoxon, log-log scaling fits) as a first-class engineering protocol, or (b) explicitly document that engineering pack = safety/V&V, and route algorithmic benchmarks to methodology/method_comparison with its engineering addendum. The router_index entry for method_comparison should add triggers like 'algorithm benchmark', 'runtime comparison', 'sorting benchmark'.

friction_items.description: methodology/method_comparison.yaml step 9 (engineering_or_systems_benchmark_addendum) contains the critical engineering-benchmark guidance (warm-up runs, CPU governor, paired Wilcoxon on heavy-tailed timings, log-log asymptotic fits, language-stdlib baseline). It is the 9th of 9 steps and starts with 'Fires when... systems-flavoured'. A naive AI walking steps top-to-bottom may have already locked in ML-vocabulary defaults (split / fold / AUROC) by step 5. The addendum should fire at step 1, not step 9.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Hoist the addendum: in step 1 (name_the_methods_and_the_task), add a branch detector that asks 'is the outcome wall-clock/throughput/latency/memory/energy?' If yes, swap the protocol's vocabulary defaults to systems-benchmark before step 2. Or split into methodology/method_comparison_ml.yaml + methodology/method_comparison_systems.yaml so the router picks the right one.

friction_items.description: method_comparison demands 'paired test that respects split structure' (Wilcoxon, McNemar, DeLong, paired t with Nadeau-Bengio correction). No MCP tool implements any of these. AI must write Python via tool_python_exec. For a naive AI without statistics background, picking the right test (Wilcoxon over t-test for heavy-tailed timings) is precisely where mistakes happen, and there is no scaffolding tool.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add tool_stats_paired_test(metric_a_path, metric_b_path, test='wilcoxon|paired_t|mcnemar|delong|nadeau_bengio') that picks defaults from data shape + writes a methods.md paragraph citing the test. Or add a protocol methodology/statistical_test_selection.yaml the router can chain after method_comparison.

friction_items.description: tool_audit_step_completeness blocks tool_synthesize when 'outputs span multiple categories (figures + tables + reports) and pipeline.yaml does not declare the sub-task DAG -- mega-scripts are blocked'. A natural benchmark runner is ONE script that loops over 3 algorithms x 5 sizes x 10 runs and emits all CSVs + figures. The audit would call this a mega-script and block synthesis. The error message in the description ('a pipeline.yaml MUST declare the sub-task DAG -- mega-scripts are blocked') is opaque -- a naive AI hitting this for the first time will not know how to write a pipeline.yaml.
friction_items.suggested_fix: When a step's only outputs come from a deterministic sweep (sweep.yaml or benchmark_config.yaml present), treat the sweep config AS the pipeline.yaml and allow the single runner script. Or auto-generate a pipeline.yaml stub from the sweep config so the gate passes. Document the pipeline.yaml schema with a benchmark example.

friction_items.description: tool_audit_step_literature blocks tool_path_finalize unless workspace/<step>/literature/findings_vs_literature.md exists with Claim/Verdict blocks. For an engineering benchmark scenario (here is my impl vs std::sort baseline), the literature loop expectation is mismatched -- the relevant 'literature' is the algorithm's canonical paper, not a meta-review of prior findings. A naive AI will either be blocked or be forced to fabricate a literature finding to satisfy the gate.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Engineering-pack steps should be exempt from step_literature gate by default, OR step_literature should accept an alternative form -- 'algorithm_provenance.md' citing the canonical paper -- as satisfying the requirement. The override_literature_gate=true escape hatch exists but a naive AI will not know to use it.

friction_items.description: tool_figure_create appears in TOOL_DEFINITIONS as a deprecation stub ('tool_figure_create was removed. Research-OS no longer ships...'). A naive AI listing tools sees this entry and may try to call it before reading the description, wasting a turn. Similarly the deprecation chatter for tool_route, tool_plan_advance, tool_search etc. lives inline as separate short entries (visible in tool name dump as 'tool_route(prompt=their message) is your 2nd -- fire them', 'tool_audit_claims (every number traces to an artefact or verified citation)', 'mem_log requires kind=...', 'sys_path requires operation=...'). These look like tool NAMES in a flat listing but are actually inlined guidance strings.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Remove deprecated tools from TOOL_DEFINITIONS entirely after one minor version; move inline guidance strings out of the tool definition dict and into sys_help topic responses. Right now they pollute the tool namespace.

friction_items.description: There is no obvious intake protocol for 'I want to run a benchmark'. The naive AI would likely pick guidance/project_startup or methodology/methodology_selection, neither of which surface the benchmark-specific intake questions (sample sizes, repetition count, what's the baseline, what hardware, what's the deployment claim). evaluation_design.yaml is closer but is framed in ML language (split, CV, leakage).
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add a thin intake protocol engineering/test/benchmark_intake.yaml that asks the right questions and routes to method_comparison with the systems-benchmark vocabulary pre-loaded.

friction_items.description: tool_route returns primary_protocol + alternatives. A naive AI typically uses primary_protocol and ignores alternatives. For this scenario, primary is likely methodology/method_comparison, alternatives may include engineering/test/build_test_fix_loop and methodology/evaluation_design -- all relevant. The naive AI loses signal by not visualising the trade-off.
friction_items.suggested_fix: tool_route response should include a 1-line 'why_this_over_alternatives' explanation per primary. Or auto-expand alternatives when the prompt has multiple plausible routes (semantic confidence is medium not high).

friction_items.description: sys_protocol_get accepts format='summary|step|full|lean|dryrun' with description noting summary or lean is recommended. The default is 'full' (3K tokens). A naive AI will probably accept the default and bloat context. The model_profile-aware default would help but is opt-in.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Change default to 'summary'; require explicit format='full' when the AI genuinely needs every step body. Or make sys_boot include current model_profile and have sys_protocol_get auto-pick lean for small models.

friction_items.description: tool_audit_reproducibility 're-runs every experiment script in a clean environment and verifies outputs match'. For a runtime benchmark, the WHOLE POINT is that timings vary; verifying they 'match' bit-for-bit will always fail. The tool has no notion of 'verify timing distribution overlaps within CI' or 'verify deterministic outputs match while allowing timing drift'.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add an output_kind='timings' mode that compares distributional summaries (median/IQR/p99) with tolerances instead of exact match. Or document explicitly that benchmark steps should be exempted and how to exempt them.

friction_items.description: The pack has tools for FMEA, FTA, requirements matrix. It has nothing for the actual artifact a benchmark produces: a runs.csv with (algorithm, size, run_id, wall_clock_ns) that needs to be aggregated into a per-(algorithm,size) median + CI + paired-difference + scaling fit. AI must write this aggregation by hand every time.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add tool_engineering_benchmark_aggregate(runs_csv, group_cols, value_col, paired_baseline) that emits a tidy summary + paired Wilcoxon + log-log scaling fit + a default winner-summary figure.

friction_items.description: tool_engineering_requirements_matrix duplicates the function of tool_audit_cross_deliverable_consistency in the requirements-tracing sense. tool_engineering_fmea_render is a one-off renderer that doesn't integrate with tool_audit_synthesis or the audit findings ledger. A naive AI doesn't know whether to trust the engineering pack tools as first-class or as ad-hoc renderers.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Wire pack tools into the audit findings ledger (write to workspace/logs/.audit_findings.jsonl) so they participate in the cross-audit story rather than living in isolation.

friction_items.description: src/research_os/server.py is 7499 lines (was 6813 at the v2 baseline kickoff). A naive AI cannot reasonably read the full server module to understand a tool's behaviour. Tool DESCRIPTIONS try to compensate but several (tool_audit_step_completeness, tool_audit_findings_query) run 200+ words of jargon that a fresh AI will not parse correctly on first read.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Phase 9 split: move TOOL_DEFINITIONS into a YAML registry; move handler dispatch into per-category modules. Tighten the longest tool descriptions to <= 80 words + link to sys_tool_describe for the full doc.

confusing_protocols: methodology/method_comparison (systems-benchmark addendum buried at step 9 of 9)
engineering/test/build_test_fix_loop (TDD-flavored; not a benchmark protocol despite living under engineering/test/)
engineering/test/test_failure_causation (5-whys + fishbone; orthogonal to a benchmark)
engineering/design/requirements_traceability (assumes SRS/SDD/V&V context that a runtime benchmark does not have)
engineering/output/engineering_report_structure (good but the Background -> Requirements -> Design -> V&V structure is awkward for a benchmark paper)
recommendations: Phase 9 consolidation MUST collapse the tool_audit_* family from 25+ entries to ~5 via a dispatcher (tool_audit_run dimension='claims|prose|figures|completeness|literature|quality'). Same for tool_dashboard_*, tool_synthesis_*, tool_search_*.

Add engineering/test/runtime_benchmark.yaml as a first-class protocol with systems-benchmark vocabulary baked in (warm-up handling, CPU governor, paired Wilcoxon, log-log scaling, language-stdlib baseline). Promote the addendum out of method_comparison step 9.
Add tool_engineering_benchmark_aggregate(runs_csv) that emits per-group summary + paired test + scaling fit + winner figure -- the missing aggregation primitive for benchmarks.
Add tool_stats_paired_test(metric_a, metric_b, test='wilcoxon|paired_t|mcnemar|delong') so AIs do not have to write statistical tests from scratch and pick wrong defaults.
Loosen tool_audit_step_completeness: a sweep.yaml or benchmark_config.yaml present should satisfy the 'pipeline.yaml required' check. Auto-generate the pipeline.yaml stub from the sweep config.
Make tool_audit_step_literature exempt for engineering-pack steps OR accept algorithm_provenance.md (citing the canonical paper) as the satisfying artifact.
Make sys_protocol_get default format='summary' instead of 'full' so naive AIs do not bloat context on first call.
Remove tool_figure_create and the other deprecation-stub entries from TOOL_DEFINITIONS once v2 ships; move inline guidance strings out of the tool dict.
tool_route response should include 'why_this_over_alternatives' per primary so naive AIs see the trade-off.
Split src/research_os/server.py (7499 lines) into a tool registry YAML + per-category handler modules. The current monolith is unreviewable by either a human or a naive AI.
